dimensions.name: Prompt Completeness
dimensions.rationale: Identity 82 words. Instructions >2000 words with 9 numbered sections covering discovery, classification, sentiment, resolution, escalation, learning, digests, weekly reports, and state management. Tool guidance documents Jira ADF format, Notion search, Slack DMs. Three rich multi-step examples. Error handling covers API failures, data integrity, and 8+ edge cases.

dimensions.name: Tool-Prompt Alignment
dimensions.rationale: http_request mapped to Jira REST, Notion REST, Slack Web API with specific endpoints and auth headers. file_read/file_write used for 4 state files. No phantom tools referenced.

dimensions.name: Trigger Coherence
dimensions.rationale: Three triggers with valid cron: 15-min polling for issue scanning, daily 9AM digest, weekly Monday 9AM health report. All align directly with prompt workflow sections.

dimensions.name: Connector Coverage
dimensions.rationale: All 3 service_flow entries (Jira, Notion, Slack) have matching connector definitions with complete credential fields, setup instructions, and API base URLs.

dimensions.name: Memory Design
dimensions.rationale: Extensive local state design: issue_states.json, solutions_kb.json, pending_confirmations.json, escalation_log.json. Self-improving knowledge base with confidence scores and confirmation tracking.

dimensions.name: Error Handling
dimensions.rationale: Per-service failure strategies with rate limit handling, state corruption recovery, duplicate action prevention, and 10+ edge cases including 100+ comment truncation, reporter-assignee conflicts, KB entry conflicts, and first-run initialization.

dimensions.name: Use Case Fidelity
dimensions.rationale: Prompt covers all use_case_flows: stale issue discovery, state classification, sentiment analysis, auto-resolution, sentiment-based routing, KB learning, daily digests, and weekly health reports.

dimensions.name: Value Clarity
dimensions.rationale: Clear business outcome: reduces stale issue backlog through auto-resolution with confidence-scored KB matching, routes by urgency with sentiment analysis, and builds institutional knowledge over time through learning loop.

dimensions.name: Execution Feasibility
dimensions.rationale: All APIs are standard REST (Jira v3, Notion v1, Slack Web API). All connectors have builtin definitions. Triggers are standard cron. File state uses simple JSON.

dimensions.name: Differentiation
dimensions.rationale: Substantial reasoning beyond automation: sentiment analysis with 1-10 scoring, confidence-scored KB matching, learning loop from confirmed resolutions, priority+sentiment routing matrix. Far beyond what a simple cron/webhook could achieve.

dimensions.name: Variable Necessity
dimensions.rationale: No explicit template variables, but adoption_questions now cover all needed config. All 8 questions target genuinely needed user-specific values.

dimensions.name: Default Quality
dimensions.rationale: Most defaults are sensible (7-day stale threshold, #engineering-alerts, 80 confidence threshold). Notion KB database_id has no default, requiring mandatory user input before first run.

issues: No adoption_questions existed before fix - Jira projects, Notion DB ID, Slack channels, thresholds, and manager email all needed user-specific configuration
Notion KB database_id has no sensible default - user must provide before first run

template: Added 8 adoption_questions: jira_projects, stale_threshold_days, slack_alert_channel, slack_escalation_channel, eng_manager_email, notion_kb_database_id, auto_close_enabled, confidence_threshold
